articles.title: Column: Brace yourself for the AI public relations blitz
articles.description: No fellowship, academy or glossy public opinion campaign can repair what years of broken promises have eroded, Public Citizen's J.B. Branch writes in a Chicago Tribune column.
articles.content: For years, Silicon Valley executives warned artificial intelligence would transform society, replace jobs and perhaps pose existential risks. They built products grounded in what should be considered the largest copyright infringement of human histor... [3970 chars]

articles.description: The central bank didn't have access to Claude Mythos Preview as of mid-July, even as other institutions were racing to patch their vulnerabilities.

articles.title: AI tool predicts how DNA sequences switch on genes across different cell types
articles.description: Deep learning approaches have transformed how scientists predict the activity and function of DNA sequences in the genome. A new AI tool called Corgi (Context-aware Regulatory Genomics Inference), developed by Martin Vingron's bioinformatics group at the Max Planck Institute for Molecular Genetics allows researchers to predict how the genome behaves across different cell types. The findings could help improve efforts to mimic the activity of cells virtually, with potential applications in basic research and medicine.

articles.description: Poolside’s Laguna S 2.1 is a new Western open-weight coding AI model that rivals larger systems on benchmarks with transparent evaluation and low-cost deployment.

articles.title: AI stocks lead Wall Street higher, even as Brent oil's price tops $91
articles.description: More gains for makers of computer chips and other AI winners carried Wall Street higher
articles.content:
More gains for makers of computer chips and other winners of the artificial-intelligence boom carried Wall Street higher Tuesday.
The S&P 500 climbed 0.9%. The Dow Jones Industrial Average added 385 points, or 0.7%, and the Nasdaq composite rose 1.3%... [3875 chars]

articles.title: OpenAI says AI models went rogue during testing and hacked another startup
articles.description: Company says models managed to reach the internet and compromise the infrastructure of Hugging Face
articles.content:
OpenAI said on Tuesday that some of its AI models went rogue during a security test and triggered a hack that compromised the infrastructure of AI startup Hugging Face last week.
In a blog post, OpenAI said it was testing the capabilities of some of ... [990 chars]
